data.title: Viscaria's environmental permit gains legal force
data.description:
The Supreme Court of Sweden today announced that it will not grant leave to appeal Viscaria's environmental permit. Today's decision means that Viscaria's environmental permit can no longer be appealed and thus gains legal force.

“We are very pleased with the court's decision. With this, we have passed the last regulatory milestone, and we can continue with full force the preparations for the reopening of the Viscaria copper mine. We now have all the permits in place to start the construction of the industrial area including the enrichment plant, and to start mining in the mine. Today's announcement is a major step towards our vision of becoming a major contributor to the growing global demand for sustainably produced copper,” says Jörgen Olsson, CEO of Viscaria.

The background

The Land and Environment Court's judgment of May 6, 2024 was appealed by Gabna Sami village, three private individuals and Viscaria to the Land and Environment Court of Appeal. On November 6, 2024, the Land and Environment Court of Appeal announced that the court does not grant leave to appeal. This decision was appealed on December 4, 2024 by Gabna Sami village to the Supreme Court, which today announced that the court does not grant leave to appeal.

data.title: M.O.B.A. Network Enhances Video Creator Services – Positioning for Growth and Stronger Margins
data.description:
Stockholm, April 16, 2025M.O.B.A. Network is actively evolving its strategy in response to changing user preferences and market dynamics, marking an important shift toward promising new growth areas, particularly within video and fintech. By rapidly adapting and prioritizing user- and creator-centric solutions, the company has identified significant opportunities to expand and diversify its revenue streams.

Union For Gamers, M.O.B.A. Network’s Multi-Channel Network (MCN) for video creators, faced volatility during 2023 and early 2024, experiencing a decrease in associated creators and significant fluctuations in views per creator. Additionally, declining revenue per view created challenging conditions for this segment. This volatility was felt broadly across the creator services landscape, with global macro-economic factors impacting consumer confidence and leading to knock-on effects across the industry, as well as a lull in trending video game releases reducing content creation opportunities among our existing creators.

In response, M.O.B.A. Network embarked on intensive development efforts during fall 2023 to strengthen its position in the video segment. A strategic partnership with Mediacube was established to revitalize Union For Gamers and enhance the overall offerings to creators. We are pleased to report that these efforts are now delivering positive results. The video segment has demonstrated consistent growth over the past three quarters with increased video views, overall watch time, and number of associated creators, validating our strategic approach. 

A particularly encouraging development has been our investment in complementary services for creators, notably within fintech. By offering industry-leading payment solutions tailored to content creators, including more ways to withdraw earnings, get paid faster, and receive advance funding, we have significantly enhanced the value proposition for our creators, while also appealing to a wider user base and creating profitable new revenue streams. Fintech services offer attractive margins and considerable future potential.

Although challenges remain, M.O.B.A. Network is leveraging valuable lessons from the past year. With a clear emphasis on strategic partnerships and innovative business models that deliver enduring value to our creators, users, and shareholders.

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 0.71% Lower
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is dropping 52 points.

Leading the losses are Stellantis NV (-3.68%), Edenred (-3.62%) and Publicis (-2.66%).

Top gainers were Carrefour (1.00%), Pernod Ricard (0.81%) and Orange (0.77%).

data.title: ABB India gains; Motilal Oswal sees higher inflows in few quarters
data.description:
** ABB India rises 2.6% to 5,381 rupees

** Stock set for third straight session of gains since releasing annual report on April 9, gaining 7% during the period; April 10 and April 14 were market holidays

** Motilal Oswal says engineering products maker's FY25 annual report highlights focus on high-growth segments and deeper penetration in tier-II cities

** Expect inflows and project ramp-ups after a few quarters

** Re-iterates "buy", with PT of 6,700 rupees

** Stock rated "buy" on avg; median PT is 6,224 rupees, per data compiled by LSEG

** YTD, ABB loses 22%

data.title: FCC Unit To Operate Urban Solid Waste Transfer Station In Minneapolis
data.description:
Fomento de Construcciones y Contratas SA :

  • UNIT WINS CONTRACT TO OPERATE URBAN SOLID WASTE TRANSFER STATION IN THE SOUTH OF MINNEAPOLIS

  • SERVICE IS EXPECTED TO LAST UP TO FIVE YEARS WITH AN ASSOCIATED PORTFOLIO OF UP TO $8.3 MILLION

data.title: ASML Fiscal Q1 Earnings, Sales Rise; Issues Q2 Sales Guidance
data.description:
ASML Holding reported Wednesday fiscal Q1 earnings of 6 euros ($6.82) per diluted share, up from 3.11 euros a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 5.74 euros.

Net sales for the quarter ended March 30 were 7.74 billion euros, up from 5.29 billion euros a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 7.77 billion euros.

The company expects Q2 net sales to range from 7.2 billion euros to 7.7 billion euros. Analysts polled by FactSet expect 7.80 billion euros.

The company maintained its 2025 sales guidance of between 30 billion euros and 35 billion euros. Analysts polled by FactSet are expecting 32.49 billion euros.

data.description:

data.title: The Calmer Co. International's Instant Kava Product to be Ranged across Woolworth Supermarkets
data.description:
The Calmer Co. International said its Fiji Kava 50 grams Instant Kava product will be ranged across the Woolsworth Group supermarket network from June, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The firm already has existing distribution in more than 800 Coles Group supermarkets and Quickstop convenience locations.

Calmer's shares jumped 25% on market close on Wednesday.

data.title: AXA'S private equity firm CAPZA in talks for majority stake in Mecadaq, a strategic player in aerospace
data.description:
CAPZA/MECADAQ:

* CAPZA, a leading private investment firm for European SMEs, announces it has entered exclusive negotiations to buy a majority stake in Mecadaq, alongside its President, Julien Dubecq, and its management team, who will significantly reinvest in the transaction.

* Mecadaq specializes in the machining of complex, high value-added detailed parts for the civil and defense aerospace sectors.

* With three production sites in France and one in the United States, the Group supports major global OEMs — including Airbus, Boeing, Dassault, Safran, Thalès — on critical programs such as A320, B737 and Rafale.

* CAPZA is part of AXA IM Alts .

data.title: Global Growth Expectations Hit 30-Year Low, BofA SurveyMarket Talk
data.description: U.S. tariffs drive global growth expectations to their lowest in 30 years, Bank of America's April global fund manager survey finds. A net 82% of respondents say the global economy is set to weaken, marking a 30-year high, BofA says in a press release accompanying the survey. Recession is likely according to 42% of respondents, while the probability of a 'hard landing'--falling inflation combined with a significant economic slowdown or recession--surges to 49% from 11% in March. Expectations of a 'soft landing'--where inflation slows without the economy slowing too much--falls to 37% from 64%, the survey says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: The DAX Index Opens 0.69% Lower
data.description:
In Frankfurt, the DAX Index is falling 146 points.

Losses were driven by Brenntag (-3.99%), Siemens Energy (-2.95%) and Daimler Truck Holding AG (-2.27%).

Biggest rises came from Sartorius (1.71%), E.ON (1.04%) and RWE (0.42%).

data.title: Stocks fall on tariff woes, baht near 6-month high
data.description:
Key points:
  • MSCI emerging Asia stocks fall
  • Stocks in Malaysia and the Philippines fall
  • Thai baht rallies to near 6-month high
  • Other Asian currencies tick higher

Stock markets in emerging Asia fell on Wednesday, snapping a four-day winning streak, as U.S. curbs on some Nvidia chip exports to China amplified trade tensions between the top economies.

Among currencies, the Thai baht surged as much as 33.22 per U.S. dollar for the first time since October 21, 2024, driven by a softer greenback, record high gold prices and China's better-than-expected economic growth in the first quarter.

Thailand is a major gold trading hub in the region, and its currency is closely linked to the commodity's price.

"The outperformance in the baht should be contributed by surging gold prices," said Poon Panichpibool, a markets strategist at Krung Thai Bank.

Analysts, however, remain cautious about short-term rallies in the baht as a correction in gold prices could pressure the currency. An escalation in the U.S.-China trade war and the knock-on effect of a weakening yuan could also dampen other emerging Asian currencies.

Panichpibool said the baht remained under pressure due to a weak tourism season, ongoing trade tensions and dividend repatriation by foreign investors.

In China, solid first-quarter growth data did little to boost domestic assets, with the onshore yuan slipping marginally and stocks falling.

China is Southeast Asia's biggest trading partner and a top investment source whose fortunes dictate growth in the developing region.

Most other emerging currencies appreciated against a subdued dollar , with the greenback clinging to mid-July lows touched last week against a basket of its peers.

The Singapore dollar and the Philippine peso firmed 0.3%, while Malaysia's ringgit moved in a tight range around its one-week high. Indonesia's rupiah ticked lower to 16,838 a dollar.

An MSCI index of world emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) was slightly lower, largely weighed down by Latin American currencies that weakened against the dollar overnight. A gauge of those currencies (.MILA00000CUS) slipped 0.3%.

In equities, the MSCI index of Asian emerging market stocks (.MIMS00000PUS) fell as much as 1.8% to snap a four-day streak. A gauge of ASEAN stocks (.MISU00000PUS) - dominated by Southeast Asian firms - also fell slightly.

Tariff volleys between the United States and China have traders fretting about global economic growth, with President Donald Trump's ever-changing policies adding to the uncertainty.

"Choppiness in markets looks to continue as the trade negotiations come underway together with the risk of more U.S. trade restriction announcements," analysts at Maybank said.

Equity benchmarks in Malaysia and the Philippines declined up to 1%, while those in Thailand and Indonesia edged lower. Singapore's FTSE Straits Times index bucked the trend, rising marginally.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Indonesia's 10-year benchmark bond yield (ID10YT=RR) slips to 6.992%

** Thailand plans to import more U.S. LNG over next 5 years, says minister

** China unexpectedly anoints new trade negotiator amid U.S. tariff war

** Indonesia to propose $10 billion US energy imports increase in tariff talks

data.title: Austria Inflation Rate Confirmed at 2.9%
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Austria slowed to 2.9% in March 2025 from 3.2% in the previous month, matching preliminary estimates and approaching the ECB’s 2% stability target.

Prices moderated mainly for clothing and footwear (0.2% vs 1.8% in February), recreation and culture (2% vs 3.3%), and restaurants and hotels (5.8% vs 6%).

Additionally, costs declined for furnishings, household equipment and maintenance (-0.9% vs -0.7%) and transport (-0.8% vs 0.8%).

Meanwhile, inflation increased further for food and non-alcoholic beverages (3.1% vs 2.6%) and alcoholic beverages and tobacco (3.7% vs 1.4%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.2% in March, following a 0.6% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: China Foreign Ministry: Urge US to stop threats and blackmail
data.description:
• Urge US to stop threats and blackmail.
  • If US wants to solve issues through dialogue, it should stop exerting maximum pressure.

data.title: NCC Lands SEK150 Million University Building Redevelopment Project in Sweden
data.description:
NCC (NCC-B.ST, NCC-A.ST) secured a turnkey project worth 150 million kronor from property owner Hemsö to redevelop Mälardalen University's "S" building in Sweden.

The Swedish construction company will convert the building into the university's administrative offices, according to a Wednesday release. Construction is slated to start in May 2025, with completion expected by the end of 2026.

data.title: Barratt Redrow's Update Bland But Welcome Given Economic BackdropMarket Talk
data.description: Barratt Redrow's update is dull and that is probably a good thing given the global market turmoil backdrop, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The company backed completions guidance for FY 2025,with its order book benefiting from house-price inflation as well as mix, Jefferies says. Still, the FTSE 100 listed house builder's slightly improving selling rates might fail to excite, with lower affordable sales continuing to constrain order book progression, Jefferies says. "Growth in the value of the private order book and evidence of house-price inflation in the order book should reassure of the robustness of customer demand," Jefferies says. Shares are up 4.40 pence, or 1%, at 432.10 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: Global View to Issue Bonds Worth Up to NT$500 Million
data.description:
Global View resolved to issue its first unsecured convertible corporate bonds, worth up to NT$500 million.

The bonds, with a three-year maturity and a zero coupon rate, will be used to finance the purchase of a logistics warehousing center.

The issuance will be underwritten by President Securities, the consumer electronic products company added.

data.title: Kerjaya Prospek Unit Bags Building Deal Worth MYR98 Million
data.description:
Kerjaya Prospek Group unit Kerjaya Prospek (M) secured a deal from Persada Mentari for the execution and completion of a building contract in Pinang, Malaysia, for 98.1 million ringgit.

The building works will be executed in two phases, according to a Tuesday Malaysian bourse filing.

The contract works will begin on April 22, and will be completed within two years, the property developer said.

data.title: PJBumi Unit, Izmir Technology Industries to Develop Drones
data.description:
PJBumi unit PJBumi Technologies signed a collaboration agreement with Izmir Technology Industries to develop and supply UAVs, drones, hydrogen power generators, and satellite launch vehicles in Malaysia, Indonesia, and globally.

Under the deal, PJBumi Technologies will have exclusive rights to commercialize Izmir Technology Industries' technologies.

The contract will be valid for five years, according to a Tuesday Malaysian bourse filing.

data.title: Shanghai Stocks Extend Rally on Strong Data
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite Index rose 0.26% to close at 3,276 on Wednesday, marking its seventh consecutive session of gains as investors reacted positively to a string of upbeat economic data.

China’s economy grew faster than expected in the first quarter, bolstered by continued policy support.

March figures for retail sales, industrial production, and fixed asset investment all exceeded market forecasts, reinforcing optimism around the country’s recovery momentum.

However, gains were tempered by renewed US-China trade tensions.

President Donald Trump ordered a probe into potential new tariffs on critical mineral imports, many of which are sourced from China, triggering concerns about a further escalation in the ongoing trade dispute.

Beijing continued to push back against Trump’s “trade bullying” as China braces for the full impact of US tariffs.

Among the session’s top performers were Cambricon Technologies (+4.2%), Ofilm Group (+4.6%), and Seres Group (+1.2%).

data.title: European Stocks Lower, ASML Tumbles
data.description:
European stocks declined on Tuesday, with both the STOXX 50 and STOXX 600 falling 0.6%, as trade war tensions persisted and investors digested a fresh round of corporate earnings.

Technology stocks were the hardest hit, led by a 6.5% drop in ASML shares after the company reported disappointing Q1 net bookings.

Its CEO also warned that “uncertainty with some of our customers” could push full-year revenue toward the lower end of guidance.

Adding further pressure to the tech and semiconductor sectors were announcements from Nvidia, revealing that the US government had blocked sales of certain AI chips to China without a license.

The company also noted it expects to take a $5.5 billion revenue hit this quarter as a result.

Basic resources and financial services also ranked among the worst-performing sectors.

data.title: UK's Bunzl hits near 4-year low after cutting outlook, halting buyback
data.description:
** Shares of business supplies distributor Bunzl slump as much as 24.4% to 2326p, their lowest since June 2021

** BNZL cuts 2025 forecast & pauses share buyback programme, citing pressure on its North American businesses & broader economic uncertainty

** As of last close, stock down 30% YTD

data.title: Blue Star Helium Reports Spudding of Colorado Helium Project Development Well
data.description:
Blue Star Helium spudded the Jackson 27 SESW 3054 development well at its Galactica helium project in Colorado, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The Jackson 27 well is anticipated to be completed and ultimately tied into production facilities, the filing said.

The shallow conductor section has been set and blowout preventers are being installed and tested to drill through the intermediate hole section, after which the well will target the Lyons Formation.

Its shares jumped 17% on market close on Wednesday.

data.title: UK medical device maker Belluscura expects 20% tariff on products sold in US
data.description:
British medical device maker Belluscura said on Wednesday that its products sold in the United States would face tariff of up to 20% and that the company was evaluating how it could cut down tariff and production costs in the long term.

The company, which focuses on developing portable oxygen technology, also announced that its finance chief, Simon Neicheril, had stepped down.

data.title: Toluene prices decline in Asia
data.description: On Tuesday, toluene prices decreased in Asia. An industry source in Asia informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices were assessed lower due to dull demand sentiments in Asia."The source added,

data.title: Taiwan Optical Platform Names Deputy COO
data.description:
Taiwan Optical Platform appointed Kuei-Lin Chiang as its new Deputy Chief Operating Officer on Tuesday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

He previously served as the General Manager of CNT CATV, it added.

data.title: REG - Strategic Minerals - Result of Placing
data.description:
RNS Number : 2663F Strategic Minerals PLC 16 April 2025  

THIS ANNOUNCEMENT AND THE INFORMATION CONTAINED HEREIN IS NOT FOR PUBLICATION, RELEASE OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN OR INTO THE UNITED STATES, AUSTRALIA, CANADA, JAPAN, THE REPUBLIC OF SOUTH AFRICA OR NEW ZEALAND OR IN OR INTO ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD CONSTITUTE A BREACH OF ANY APPLICABLE LAW OR REGULATION.

THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS FOR INFORMATION PURPOSES ONLY AND DOES NOT ITSELF CONSTITUTE A PROSPECTUS OR OFFERING MEMORANDUM OR AN OFFER FOR SALE OR SUBSCRIPTION IN RESPECT OF ANY SECURITIES IN THE COMPANY. THIS ANNOUNCEMENT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE OR CONTAIN ANY INVITATION, SOLICITATION, RECOMMENDATION, OFFER OR ADVICE TO ANY PERSON TO SUBSCRIBE FOR, OTHERWISE ACQUIRE OR DISPOSE OF ANY SECURITIES OF STRATEGIC MINERALS PLC IN ANY JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD BREACH ANY APPLICABLE LAW OR REGULATION.

THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED BY THE COMPANY TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW PURSUANT TO THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, AS AMENDED. UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INFORMATION IS CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.

16 April 2025

Strategic Minerals Plc

("Strategic Minerals" or the "Group")

Result of Placing

Oversubscribed Placing with strong investor support

 to accelerate development of the Redmoor Tungsten-Tin-Copper project, Cornwall

Strategic Minerals plc (AIM: SML; USOTC: SMCDF), an international mineral exploration and production company, announces that, further to its announcement of 7.00 a.m. today (the "Announcement"), it has successfully completed and closed the Placing.

The Placing, which was oversubscribed, has raised, in aggregate, gross proceeds of £1m through the placing of 333,333,333 new Ordinary Shares to certain investors at a price of 0.3 pence per share (the "Placing Shares"). The Placing Price represents a discount of 25 per cent. to the Closing Price of 0.4 pence per Ordinary Share on 15 April 2025, being the latest practicable business day prior to the publication of the Announcement.

The net proceeds of the Placing will be principally used to progress activities at the Company's Redmoor Tungsten-Tin-Copper Project in Cornwall and for working capital purposes.

The allotment and issue of the Placing Shares is conditional, inter alia, upon:

•      the passing, without amendment, of the Resolutions at the General Meeting;

•      Admission of the Placing Shares becoming effective by no later than 8.00 a.m. on 6 May 2025 (or such later time and / or date as the Company and the Joint Bookrunners shall agree, not being later than 30 May 2025);

•      the delivery by the Company to the Joint Bookrunners of certain documents required under the Placing Agreement;

•      the Company having fully performed its obligations under the Placing Agreement to the extent that such obligations fall to be performed prior to Admission of the Placing Shares; and

•      the Placing Agreement not having been terminated in accordance with its terms prior to Admission.

A notice convening the General Meeting is expected to be issued shortly.

Charles Manners, Chairman of Strategic Minerals, commented:

"We are delighted with the response from investors to the opportunity to accelerate development of a potentially world class, high-grade, polymetallic tungsten project right here in the UK.

"The oversubscribed Placing wholly supports the match grant funding from the UK Government announced last week, delivering Strategic Minerals' shareholders over £1.5 million of exploration budget over the next 12 months, culminating in the formulation of an investment-ready business case and delivery plan for moving Redmoor toward prefeasibility.

"Tungsten is essential in defence, electronics, and manufacturing industries and recent global events have underscored the importance of diversification of supply. We look forward to sharing progress on the work programme as we focus on scaling the opportunity in Cornwall."

Admission and Total Voting Rights

Subject to the Resolutions being passed at the General Meeting, application will be made to the London Stock Exchange for admission of the Placing Shares to trading on AIM ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and dealings in the Placing Shares will commence on AIM at 8.00 a.m. on or around 6 May 2025 (or such later date as may be agreed between the Company and the Joint Bookrunners, but no later than 30 May 2025).

The Placing Shares will be issued fully paid and will rank pari passu in all respects with the Company's existing Ordinary Shares.

Following Admission, the total number of Ordinary Shares in the capital of the Company in issue will be 2,349,297,949 with voting rights. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company's issued share capital pursuant to the Company's Articles.

Capitalised terms used but not otherwise defined in this announcement shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Announcement, unless the context requires otherwise.

Notes to Editors

About Strategic Minerals Plc

Strategic Minerals plc (AIM: SML; USOTC: SMCDY) is an AIM-quoted, producing minerals company, actively developing strategic projects in the UK, United States and Australia.

In 2019, the Company completed the 100% acquisition of Cornwall Resources Limited ("CRL") and the Redmoor Tungsten-Tin-Copper Project.

The Redmoor Project is situated within the historically significant Tamar Valley Mining District in Cornwall, United Kingdom, with a JORC Compliant (2012) Inferred Mineral Resource Estimate published 14 February 2019:

WO3(EQ)% = Sn% x 0.7 + WO3 + Cu% x 0.28.  Commodity price assumptions: WO3 US$ 33,000/t, Sn US$ 22,000/t, Cu US$ 7,000/t.  Recovery assumptions: total WO3 recovery 72%, total Sn recovery 68% & total Cu recovery 85% and payability assumptions of 81%, 90% and 90% respectively

More information on Cornwall Resources can be found at: https://www.cornwallresources.com

In September 2011, Strategic Minerals acquired the distribution rights to the Cobre magnetite project in New Mexico, USA, through its wholly owned subsidiary Southern Minerals Group ("SMG").  Cobre has been in production since 2012 and continues to provide a sustainable revenue stream for the Company.

In March 2018, the Company completed the acquisition of the Leigh Creek Copper Mine ("LCCM") situated in the copper rich belt of South Australia.  The Company continues seek opportunities to monetise the asset.
